D1 Optics L1 Identify Atom

Light Takes Time

watch a pulse travel from the sun to distant planets at exactly 299,792 km/s

Light moves at a finite speed — fast enough to feel instant, slow enough that the sun you see is already eight minutes in the past.

Slide the target distance from Mercury (0.39 AU) to Neptune (30 AU). The gold pulse marches outward at exactly c — its journey takes minutes for inner planets, hours for outer ones.
